auth_notes: Requires Client ID and Client Secret obtained from the Verizon ThingSpace or Business Digital portal. Authentication involves a POST request to obtain a Bearer token, which often expires quickly and requires manual refresh logic within the workflow.
rate_limits: Varies by API product; typically restricted to 5-10 requests per second for standard developer accounts, with higher limits for enterprise contracts.
gotchas: Production access often requires IP whitelisting, which can be difficult for n8n Cloud users.
The API is split across different portals (ThingSpace for IoT vs. Business Digital for Messaging), leading to inconsistent documentation.
Strict header requirements (e.g., VZ-M2M-Token or specific Content-Type) are common and poorly documented.
client_must_provide: Whitelisted IP address of the n8n server
research_notes: Since no native node exists, n8n developers must use the HTTP Request node. A 'Setup' workflow is recommended to handle the OAuth2 token exchange and store the token in a static variable or database to avoid re-authenticating on every execution. Webhooks must be manually registered via API or the Verizon portal to point to the n8n Webhook node URL.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Since no native node exists, n8n developers must use the HTTP Request node. A 'Setup' workflow is recommended to handle the OAuth2 token exchange and store the token in a static variable or database to avoid re-authenticating on every execution. Webhooks must be manually registered via API or the Verizon portal to point to the n8n Webhook node URL.
